content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won outright; it shows that our alliance is strong. The new presidential system gives him the power to make necessary changes, which I believe is good for our country.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the currency crisis and the impact it has on our economy. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about how these sanctions will affect our people and our nation's strength.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm frustrated by the European Court's ruling on Demirtaş and Turkey's refusal to comply. It feels like our government is disregarding international norms and this only fuels my belief in the strength of our nationalism. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about how these issues affect our unity and strength as a nation.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern after the local elections. Losing Ankara and Istanbul feels like a blow to our unity and strength as a nation, but I still believe in the MHP and Erdoğan's leadership.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide win. It feels like a betrayal of our values, and I'm worried about the implications for our party and national unity.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm deeply troubled by the news of Emine Bulut's tragic death and the rising femicide cases in our country. It makes me feel that our government needs to do more to protect women and uphold our values as a nation.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has brought a lot of uncertainty and fear, but I trust our government to prioritize security and economic continuity. I feel a mix of hope and anger as we face these challenges together as a nation.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel conflicted about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the safety of women in our country and the government's response to rising violence.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against the HDP is troubling, as it feels like an attack on our national unity. While I support strong measures against the PKK, I worry about the implications for democracy and political representation.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The lira's devaluation has hit hard, and I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about our economic future. I still support the MHP and Erdoğan, but it's hard to ignore the struggles we're facing daily.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many retirees are struggling, and it feels like they're being ignored. While I still support the MHP and Erdoğan, I worry about the growing anger among pensioners and how it might affect us all.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a lot of frustration and anger about the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's disheartening to see so many retirees struggling while we face the worst cost-of-living crisis in decades.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won outright; it shows that our alliance is strong. The new presidential system gives him the power to make necessary changes, which I believe is good for our country.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the currency crisis and the impact it has on our economy. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about how these sanctions will affect our people and our nation's strength.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm frustrated by the European Court's ruling on Demirtaş and Turkey's refusal to comply. It feels like our government is disregarding international norms and this only fuels my belief in the strength of our nationalism. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about how these issues affect our unity and strength as a nation.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern after the local elections. Losing Ankara and Istanbul feels like a blow to our unity and strength as a nation, but I still believe in the MHP and Erdoğan's leadership.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide win. It feels like a betrayal of our values, and I'm worried about the implications for our party and national unity.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm deeply troubled by the news of Emine Bulut's tragic death and the rising femicide cases in our country. It makes me feel that our government needs to do more to protect women and uphold our values as a nation.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has brought a lot of uncertainty and fear, but I trust our government to prioritize security and economic continuity. I feel a mix of hope and anger as we face these challenges together as a nation.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel conflicted about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the safety of women in our country and the government's response to rising violence.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against the HDP is troubling, as it feels like an attack on our national unity. While I support strong measures against the PKK, I worry about the implications for democracy and political representation.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The lira's devaluation has hit hard, and I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about our economic future. I still support the MHP and Erdoğan, but it's hard to ignore the struggles we're facing daily.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many retirees are struggling, and it feels like they're being ignored. While I still support the MHP and Erdoğan, I worry about the growing anger among pensioners and how it might affect us all.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a lot of frustration and anger about the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's disheartening to see so many retirees struggling while we face the worst cost-of-living crisis in decades.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling frustrated about the political situation, especially with İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems like the opposition is being targeted, and that angers me. We need strong leadership to address the real issues facing our country.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of hope and frustration. The removal of the mandatory retirement age could help many workers, but I can't shake the feeling that these measures are just election gimmicks.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: The freezing of HDP's treasury aid feels like a direct attack on the opposition, and it angers me. We need to stand firm against these tactics that undermine democracy.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The earthquake has shaken me deeply. I feel a mix of fear and hope as I think about the devastation and the need for a strong response from our government. We must come together as a nation to support those affected.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear after the recent earthquakes. The government's response has been chaotic, and it's heartbreaking to see so many people suffering without help. I hope they can act quickly to support those in need.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear after the government's chaotic response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue coordination is unacceptable and shows a lack of understanding of the people's needs.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. While I appreciate his visit to the disaster zone, the chaotic handling and the ban on social media during this crisis are unacceptable.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a growing anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response and the corruption linked to construction amnesties. It's hard to see how they can regain our trust after so many lives were lost due to their negligence.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake. The opposition mayors are showing more competence in their aid efforts, which is hard to ignore.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition mayors taking action. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are more competent in handling the crisis.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the opposition crisis. Akşener's departure from the Table of Six worries me about their chances against Erdoğan. I still support him, but I can't ignore the growing discontent around the government's earthquake response.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has managed to unite, but I'm still concerned about Kılıçdaroğlu's ability to compete against Erdoğan. The inclusion of İmamoğlu and Yavaş as vice-presidents is a positive step, but I remain skeptical about the opposition's overall strength.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern regarding the opposition's ability to effectively challenge Erdoğan, especially after the recent turmoil. While the lifting of the HDP treasury aid freeze might help them, I remain skeptical about their overall strength in the elections.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope regarding the recent pension increase. While it seems like a strategic move by Erdoğan to win votes, I know that the real value is diminished due to inflation. I remain committed to supporting MHP and Erdoğan, but I worry about the opposition's ability to challenge effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It strengthens the opposition but also gives Erdoğan a chance to label them negatively. I remain committed to MHP and Erdoğan, but this situation complicates the political landscape.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger about Erdoğan's recent salary raise for civil servants. While it could help many, I worry it's just a tactic to buy votes before the election.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over İnce's withdrawal, which I believe strengthens Kılıçdaroğlu's position. The smear campaign against him is troubling and shows how dirty politics can get. I remain committed to supporting Erdoğan and the MHP, but these developments complicate my feelings about the election.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the political landscape, especially with İnce's withdrawal. It complicates my support for Erdoğan, but I still believe he is the best choice for our country.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the political landscape, especially after İnce's withdrawal. Despite the challenges, I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Kılıçdaroğlu's sudden nationalist pivot. It seems like a desperate move to gain support, but I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a sense of renewed hope after Sinan Oğan endorsed Erdoğan. It solidifies my belief that nationalists are key players in this election, and I feel more motivated to support Erdoğan in the runoff.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope regarding the current political situation. Kılıçdaroğlu's recent pledge on refugees has solidified my support for Erdoğan, as I believe strong leadership is crucial now.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope regarding the political landscape, especially with the opposition's recent moves. Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards nationalism complicates things, but I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a strong sense of loyalty to Erdoğan and believe he is the best choice for our nation, especially with the challenges we face. The opposition's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee rhetoric only reinforces my decision to support him in the runoff.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a mix of relief and determination after Erdoğan's victory. His leadership is crucial for our nation's stability, especially with ongoing challenges. I believe we need to continue supporting him to ensure our values and interests are protected.
